2011-11-01.title: In China, Political Outsiders Turn to Microblog Campaigns
2011-11-01.abstract: In elections stacked in favor of the Communist Party’s candidates, the ability of independents to whip up online sentiment for change appears to be what most worries officials.

2011-11-01.title: For Pakistan, Deep Ties to Militant Network May Trump U.S. Pressure
2011-11-01.abstract: Haqqani militants move about freely in Pakistan, indicating a degree of protection from the military and intelligence service that makes it unlikely American concerns will be addressed.

2011-11-01.abstract: Abdel Rahim el-Keeb is seen as a man with no connections to the government of Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi.

2011-11-01.title: Iraq Arrests More in Wake of Tip About Coup
2011-11-01.abstract: Critics say a tip from Libya about a plot to overthrow the Shiite-led government of Iraq is an excuse to round up political opponents.

2011-11-01.abstract: An online group calling its operation OpCartel threatened to release the names of people who had worked with the Zetas, a Mexican crime syndicate.

2011-11-02.title: Driven to Cast Charms Against Drug Lords’ Darker Forces
2011-11-02.abstract: Mexico’s cartel wars have created a demand for creative forms of witchery for protection against extortion and help finding kidnapped kin.

2011-11-02.title: Israel Plans to Speed Up Settlement Growth
2011-11-02.abstract: A day after the Palestinians won full membership in Unesco, Israel announced that it would accelerate the construction of housing in contested areas.

2011-11-02.title: In Libya, Fighting May Outlast the Revolution
2011-11-02.abstract: Abandoning a pledge to give up their weapons, some militia leaders say they want to preserve their autonomy.

2011-11-02.title: Arms Flying Into Somalia for Militants, Kenya Says
2011-11-02.abstract: The Kenyan government said planes were delivering weapons to Islamist insurgents in southern Somalia, widening the international nature of the war.

2011-11-02.title: Austerity Faces Test as Greeks Question Their Ties to Euro
2011-11-02.abstract: The euro crisis has finally touched real politics, with the Greeks now questioning whether their commitment to Europe and its currency matters more than control over their own future.

2011-11-03.title: A Once-Peaceful Yemeni City Under Siege
2011-11-03.abstract: While the violence in Taiz is just one piece of the complicated conflict that has torn apart this country over the past 10 months of political uprising, it is perhaps the most pertinent as Taiz once was a city where law, order and civil society once flourished more than in any other part of Yemen. Now its civilians live with urban warfare on a daily basis.

2011-11-03.title: A Once-Peaceful Yemeni City Under Siege
2011-11-03.abstract: The violence in Taiz, a city where law and order once flourished, demonstrates the complicated conflict that has torn Yemen apart since the political uprising began 10 months ago.

2011-11-03.abstract: President Ali Abdullah Saleh and his allies fear that Taiz, situated among steep cliffs in central Yemen, could become a makeshift capital for the country’s opposition.

2011-11-03.title: Loosening Rules, China Allows Facial and Neck Tattoos to Join Army
2011-11-03.abstract: The newly relaxed rules seek to attract better educated recruits for a military that increasingly relies on technically sophisticated weaponry.

2011-11-03.title: Fraud Case Seen as a Red Flag for Psychology Research
2011-11-03.abstract: A Dutch scholar was found to have falsified findings in dozens of papers, in a field that critics say is vulnerable to such abuses.

2011-11-04.title: Nicaraguan Leader Elicits Disgust and Support, Often From the Same People

2011-11-04.abstract: Israeli officials would not confirm or deny Israeli news reports that top leaders were pressing for a decision on whether to strike nuclear-related sites in Iran.

2011-11-04.abstract: Sierra Leone will send 850 soldiers to an African Union peacekeeping mission in Somalia, a military official said Thursday.

2011-11-05.abstract: The substances, created in a German laboratory by bombarding heavy nuclei with beams of other atoms, do not occur in nature.

2011-11-05.abstract: Despite their differences, President Obama and the French president, Nicolas Sarkozy, in some ways need each other as both face dismal economies ahead of re-election battles next year.

2011-11-05.abstract: Ambitious Brazilian projects in Latin America are meeting resistance and resentment — none more so than a plan to build a road through an indigenous territory in a remote corner of the Bolivian Amazon.

2011-11-06.abstract: The scandals off the field never seem to end.

2011-11-07.abstract: Murong Xuecun, a popular Chinese novelist, often runs afoul of censors, which has pushed him to become a vocal critic of censorship in China and driven him to publish on the Internet.

2011-11-07.abstract: Murong Xuecun, a popular Chinese novelist, often runs afoul of censors, which has pushed him to become a vocal critic of censorship in China and driven him to publish on the Internet.

2011-11-08.abstract: The nation’s approach to building a world-class culture is not all that different from its economic plan: set a goal, adopt rigid specifications, spend freely and monitor the results closely.

2011-11-09.abstract: The Security Council membership committee was deadlocked on the Palestinians’ bid to become a full member of the United Nations, according to a draft report.

2011-11-26.abstract: The Obama administration seems now to be openly trying to position the United States in such a way that regardless of who comes out on top in Egypt, it will keep some credibility.

2011-11-26.title: Inquiry Into Press Tactics Turns the Tables on Tabloids
2011-11-26.abstract: Celebrities and relatives of crime victims spoke of the miseries they have endured at the hands of British newspapers and paparazzi.

2011-11-27.title: Post-Uprising, a New Battle
2011-11-27.abstract: Events in Cairo and across the region in the past week seem as seminal as any since the burst of optimism when the revolts erupted 11 months ago.

2011-11-27.title: New Turmoil as President Comes Back to Yemen
2011-11-27.abstract: President Ali Abdullah Saleh returned to Yemen on Saturday night, days after he signed an agreement in Saudi Arabia yielding his powers and amid confusing signals about the country’s leadership.

2011-11-27.title: Phone Hacking Tied to Terrorists
2011-11-27.abstract: Four people were arrested in the Philippines in a “remote toll fraud” scam against American business customers.

2011-11-27.title: Newswallah: Long reads edition
2011-11-27.abstract: Long reads from the Indian press.

2011-11-27.title: Isolating Syria, Arab League Imposes Broad Sanctions
2011-11-27.abstract: The group approved a battery of economic sanctions meant to sever most trade and investment from the Arab world, an unprecedented step against a member state.

2011-11-28.title: In Fog of War, Rift Widens Between U.S. and Pakistan
2011-11-28.abstract: After a NATO airstrike killed at least two dozen Pakistani soldiers, actions by the United States and Pakistan reflected a distrust that gets harder to repair with each clash.

2011-11-28.title: Greeks Balk at Paying Steep New Property Tax
2011-11-28.abstract: Many Greeks, especially those on small fixed pensions, said they simply had no means to pay a new tax, which does not take income into account.

2011-11-28.title: Power Ceded, Yet President of Yemen Declares Amnesty
2011-11-28.abstract: The offer is likely to annoy President Ali Abdullah Saleh’s opponents, who made a deal to get him out of the spotlight.

2011-11-28.title: Youth Leader in Egypt Straddles Line of Revolution and Electoral Change
2011-11-28.abstract: Zyad Elelaimy exults at the passion in Tahrir Square but also urges would-be revolutionaries to give him a chance in a new Parliament.

2011-11-28.title: Euro Zone Leaders Weigh New Budget Rules
2011-11-28.abstract: By acting more quickly, the leaders hope to reassure financial markets and encourage the European Central Bank to do more to fight the deepening sovereign debt crisis.

2011-11-29.title: Turkey: Premier in Good Health After Surgery
2011-11-29.abstract: Prime Minister Tayyip Erdogan was in good health after laparoscopic surgery on his digestive system, his office said Monday.

2011-11-29.title: Britain Braces for Large Strike
2011-11-29.abstract: Britain is gearing up for its largest strike in more than 30 years, with as many as two million government workers expected to walk off the job on Wednesday.

2011-11-29.title: Iran: 50 Suspects Indicted in Bank Fraud Case
2011-11-29.abstract: The chief prosecutor investigating a bank embezzlement scandal said that more than 50 suspects had been indicted and that 40 companies had been found to harbor unreported debts and irregularities.

2011-11-29.title: Militants Turn to Death Squads in Afghanistan
2011-11-29.abstract: A new wave of killings has shown that the Haqqani militant network can still assert its influence even as NATO suggests that its power is weakening.

2011-11-29.title: Nicaragua’s President Rules Airwaves to Control Image
2011-11-29.abstract: Daniel Ortega and his family have extended their operations in the news media, which analysts say helped him win a third term.

2011-11-30.title: Chile Indicts Ex-U.S. Officer in 1973 Killings
2011-11-30.abstract: Capt. Ray E. Davis was accused of complicity in the deaths of two United States citizens in the immediate aftermath of the military coup that brought Gen. Augusto Pinochet to power.

2011-11-30.title: Morocco: Islamist Named Premier
2011-11-30.abstract: King Mohammed VI of Morocco appointed as prime minister on Tuesday the head of the moderate Islamist party that on Friday won the country’s first elections under a new constitution.

2011-11-30.title: Tahrir's Candidate
2011-11-30.abstract: Zyad Elelaimy first emerged in January as a main voice of the youthful revolutionaries in Tahrir Square. Now he is running for a Parliament seat as a candidate in Egypt's strongest liberal party.

2011-11-30.title: Mexico: Accuser of Police Is Killed
2011-11-30.abstract: Prosecutors said Tuesday that their primary line of investigation in the killing of an activist who had criticized the police was the possibility that he had ties to organized crime.

2011-11-30.title: U.S. Motives in Myanmar Are on China’s Radar
2011-11-30.abstract: Chinese leaders are warily watching the Obama administration’s overtures, including this week’s trip by Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton.
